fallacy_name: Lean 4 Syntax and Logic Failure (Broken Cut-and-Paste Formalization)
attack_vector: The Lean 4 proofs are syntactically invalid and mathematically flawed. In the second block, `cut_paste_property` is defined as `CS(x1,y1) = CS(x2,y2) → Out(x1,y1) = Out(x2,y2)`, which merely states that the crossing sequence determines the output, completely missing the crucial mixed-input property of the Cut-and-Paste lemma. Consequently, the proof of `FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound` fails: `apply cut_paste_property` generates the goal `CS(p.1, q.1) = CS(p.1, p.2)`, but the proof attempts to close it with `exact h_cross_eq` (which is `CS(p.1, p.2) = CS(q.1, q.2)`). This is a blatant type mismatch. Furthermore, in the first block, `h_cut (p1.1, p1.2) (p1.1, p2.2) p1 p2` passes pairs to arguments expecting individual elements of `X` and `Y`, causing another type error.
director_pivot_advice: You must correctly formalize the Cut-and-Paste lemma. The correct property is: `CS(x1, y1) = CS(x2, y2) → CS(x1, y2) = CS(x1, y1) ∧ Out(x1, y2) = Out(x1, y1)`. Then, in your proof, use this property to show that if `CS(a,a) = CS(b,b)`, then `Out(a,b) = Out(a,a)`. Fix the type mismatches by passing the correct individual arguments (e.g., `h_cut p1.1 p1.2 p2.1 p2.2`) instead of tuples. Ensure your Lean code actually compiles.
